title: Legacy Nagios, Icinga, Zabbix, and Nimsoft Alert Ingestion Health, Cross-Tool Deduplication, and Source-of-Truth Reconciliation

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — service entities keyed on normalized_host when KPIs consume the same alert indexes

dataSources: `index=monitoring` and `index=legacy_alerts` carrying TA-normalized lanes: `sourcetype=nagios:nagios.log`, `sourcetype=nagios:host_perfdata`, `sourcetype=zabbix:event`, `sourcetype=zabbix:problem`, `sourcetype=icinga2:event`, `sourcetype=solarwinds:netflow`, `sourcetype=nimsoft:alarm`, `sourcetype=scom:alert`; lookup `legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv` keyed on `canonical_host`, `host_fqdn`, and `ip_primary` for owner and environment enrichment.
app: Splunk App for Observability or Splunk Enterprise platform processing with Splunk Add-on for Nagios Core (Splunkbase), Zabbix Add-on For Splunk (Splunkbase), Icinga2 Passive Check integrations, SolarWinds Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase) where NPM exports are used, plus Universal Forwarder syslog and SNMP trap receivers
description: Detects legacy on-premises monitoring ingestion gaps, cross-tool duplicate fan-out, source-of-truth severity conflicts, and heartbeat silence when Nagios Core or XI, Icinga 2, Zabbix, SolarWinds NPM-related feeds, CA Nimsoft DX UIM, and Microsoft SCOM alerts land in Splunk alongside CIM Alerts recency so platform teams can correlate classic NMS signals with modern observability workflows.
value: Reliability and infrastructure leaders regain confidence that brownfield monitoring consoles and Splunk agree during incidents, reducing pager noise from duplicate routes while still catching true multi-tool disagreement, collector starvation, and quiet forwarders before customers notice blind spots.
grandmaExplanation: We pull bells from several old control rooms into one timeline so crews can see when two rooms disagree about the same machine, when a room falls quiet without permission, or when the same fault rings twice through different doors.
implementation: Land Nagios Core TA feeds and Zabbix normalization into index=monitoring or index=legacy_alerts with sourcetypes nagios:nagios.log, nagios:host_perfdata, zabbix:event, and zabbix:problem; forward Icinga2, SolarWinds NPM NetFlow heartbeats as solarwinds:netflow, Nimsoft nimsoft:alarm, and SCOM scom:alert; maintain legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv; schedule observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation every ten minutes over earliest=-4h@h; route page rows to platform monitoring on-call; archive weekly CSV extracts to your evidence index.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the enterprise monitoring leadership that still operates Nagios Core or Nagios XI schedulers, Icinga 2 masters and satellites, Zabbix server and proxy fleets, CA UIM Nimsoft robots and hubs, Microsoft SCOM management groups, and SolarWinds NPM collectors that often sit beside older syslog and SNMP trap concentrators. This use case is deliberately about the messy middle of hybrid observability: legacy network and systems management tools continue to emit authoritative host and service state for brownfield estates, while Splunk-native observability, ITSI services, and OpenTelemetry pipelines become the analytical home for triage. The detection therefore measures four intertwined risks at once: ingestion gaps where a forwarder, trap listener, or API poller stops delivering events while operators still assume coverage; duplicate fan-out where the same underlying fault surfaces through multiple tools with slightly different naming; source-of-truth reconciliation where one tool insists a host check is critical while another reports recovery or OK within the same correlation window; and heartbeat silence where a tool that normally chatters every few minutes goes quiet for longer than the agreed SLA without an approved maintenance annotation.

Scope boundaries keep this UC distinct from sibling cat-13 observability content. UC-13.3.5 remains the home for alert storm economics across paging vendors, webhook deduplication keys, and episode queue floods rather than per-host reconciliation across Nagios-class platforms. UC-13.3.15 remains the home for OpenTelemetry Collector queue pressure and exporter backpressure rather than syslog-fed Nagios or Zabbix notification streams. UC-13.3.1 answers whether your consolidated Splunk indexes still reflect what those legacy platforms believe about hosts and services, and whether multi-tool overlap is creating operator confusion during incidents. Splunk becomes the tamper-evident ledger that fuses TA-normalized sourcetype lanes with inventory-backed host keys so responders stop arguing about which console is right during a bridge call.

Nagios Core and XI deployments typically emit host and service state through nagios.log style files, perfdata files, and notification commands that can be mirrored into Splunk with the Splunk Add-on for Nagios Core patterns or scripted inputs that preserve ordering. Icinga 2 clusters emit structured events that map cleanly to icinga2:event when you forward JSON or key-value syslog from feature streams. Zabbix problem and event feeds land as zabbix:problem and zabbix:event once HTTP pollers or syslog bridges normalize triggers and item keys. SolarWinds NPM environments may contribute interface and flow-adjacent telemetry including solarwinds:netflow rows that still act as a freshness heartbeat for collector hosts even when alert text lives in parallel indexes. CA Nimsoft DX UIM robots raise nimsoft:alarm rows with probe and origin metadata that must align to CMDB hostnames. SCOM management servers forward scom:alert events with MonitoringObjectDisplayName and resolution_state semantics that differ from Unix-centric Nagios wording.

RBAC and credential hygiene matter as much as parsing. Read-only service accounts should poll Nagios NDOUTILs or Zabbix APIs without interactive admin passwords on laptops. SNMP trap receivers and syslog relays must run on hardened hosts with segregated network paths so a single compromised VLAN cannot spoof every legacy alert. Store Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens and modular input passwords in credential stores with rotation contracts. Legal reviewers should confirm hostnames, IP addresses, and customer identifiers appearing in alarm text meet retention and minimization policies before landing raw payloads in shared indexes.

Index design: designate index=monitoring for primary TA-fed lanes when you want a single analyst-facing namespace, and index=legacy_alerts when compliance or network segmentation requires separate retention for third-party monitoring exports. Hot retention should cover at least fourteen days for incident replay, thirty days for cross-tool reconciliation studies, and cold storage aligned to observability governance. Stamp change_ticket_id and maintenance_window_id on forwarded payloads whenever platform teams pause checks or drain traps during approved work so downstream severity logic can downgrade expected silence.

Field normalization: coalesce host, hostname, dest, robot, origin, and MonitoringObjectDisplayName into normalized_host using inventory lookups rather than naive lowercase only. Map service, trigger_name, check_name, probe, and AlertName into check_key strings that survive whitespace and version upgrades. Map heterogeneous severity vocabularies into numeric sev_num tiers so case() blocks can compare Nagios CRITICAL against Zabbix Disaster and SCOM Error without string brittleness. Preserve legacy_tool labels per multisearch arm so deduplication math never confuses which platform spoke last.

Risk briefing: a quiet legacy lane during maintenance can look like catastrophe if you forget to tag the silence. Conversely, aggressive deduplication without inventory joins can hide genuine multi-datacenter incidents when the same FQDN appears twice with different serial numbers. SolarWinds NetFlow rows are not a substitute for full NPM alert coverage but they help detect collector starvation when flow stops while other tools still claim health. Nimsoft and SCOM often disagree with Nagios on Linux agents versus Windows agents; reconciliation requires explicit ownership of golden host keys in the CMDB, not Splunk alone.

Collector posture: Universal Forwarders on syslog relays should use persistent queues and disk quotas that survive indexer maintenance windows. Heavy forwarders receiving SNMP traps must monitor drop counters on the OS and network interface alongside Splunk internal logs. When Splunk shows lagging _time but fresh file modtimes on Nagios logs, suspect time zone offsets or daylight saving misconfiguration on the source.

Differentiation recap: legacy NMS ingest health, cross-tool deduplication, source-of-truth conflict detection, and heartbeat silence for Nagios-class estates, not pure OTel collector saturation, not paging vendor storm economics in isolation, not Kubernetes pod-only analytics without legacy monitoring context.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands Nagios Core and XI exports with sourcetype=nagios:nagios.log for state transitions and notifications plus sourcetype=nagios:host_perfdata when perfdata files are indexed for latency and reachability context. Install or reference the Splunk Add-on for Nagios Core on heavy forwarders that can read log paths or poll NDOUTIL databases according to your architecture. Preserve host_name, service_description, state, and plugin_output fields for correlation_key construction. When Nagios XI centralizes many distributed instances, include instance_id or site labels so identical service names on different masters do not collide incorrectly.

Lane B captures Zabbix with sourcetype=zabbix:event for event stream history and sourcetype=zabbix:problem for open problem objects when your TA or webhook normalizer splits them. Map triggerid, r_eventid, host, name, severity, and operational data fields. Align clocks so Zabbix problem start times match Splunk _time within one polling interval. Volume-control high-churn discovery items by routing lab proxies to non-production indexes.

Lane C forwards Icinga 2 check results and notifications into sourcetype=icinga2:event using the JSON log feature, syslog, or a REST poller that emits one JSON object per line. Retain host, service, state, and check_command attributes. Satellites should tag zone or endpoint metadata so duplicate paths through parent zones can be distinguished during dedupe review.

Lane D brings SolarWinds NPM-related telemetry including sourcetype=solarwinds:netflow for flow records that prove collector and exporter freshness on interfaces NPM manages. When you also ingest SolarWinds alerts through a separate sourcetype, keep both lanes in the same index family so multisearch can fan without brittle index wildcards. Document which NPM nodes act as trap forwarders so silence on those hosts pages network operations first.

Lane E indexes CA UIM Nimsoft alarms as sourcetype=nimsoft:alarm with robot, probe, supp_key, and severity fields preserved. Normalize origin to the same canonical_host values your CMDB expects for that data center. Lane F indexes Microsoft SCOM alerts as sourcetype=scom:alert with MonitoringObjectDisplayName, AlertName, Severity, and ResolutionState extracted. Forward via SCOM connector, syslog, or a supported TA pattern consistent with your security architecture.

Maintain legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv with columns canonical_host, host_fqdn, ip_primary, owner_team, environment, nms_tier, and monitoring_role refreshed nightly from CMDB exports. The monitoring_role column should record whether the host is primarily watched by Nagios, Zabbix, Icinga, NPM, Nimsoft, or SCOM so silent_tool_risk logic can respect intentional single-tool ownership. Maintain a parallel maintenance_calendar lookup if you need automated suppression beyond simple tags.

props.conf should set appropriate LINE_BREAKER and TIMESTAMP_FORMAT rules for each sourcetype, especially multiline Nagios plugin output and Zabbix JSON. Use FIELDALIAS and EVAL in transforms to align dest and host when CIM Alerts mapping is enabled for downstream Enterprise Security or ITSI consumers. KV_MODE=json for structured Icinga and Zabbix payloads reduces regex fragility.

Example design-review helpers from bastion hosts with credentials:

```bash
tail -n 200 /var/log/nagios/nagios.log
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer REDACTED" https://zabbix.example/api_jsonrpc.php -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"problem.get","params":{"recent":true},"id":1}'
icinga2ctl list objects type Host --name '*.example.com' | head
```

Pre-save validation: each sourcetype must return non-zero events in a one-hour steady-state window on production-equivalent indexes before enabling the scheduled search. Negative tests should confirm decommissioned hosts disappear from inventory lookups within one refresh cycle.

Dashboards: Splunk App for Observability or Splunk Enterprise operational dashboards should expose per-lane volume, last_seen timestamps by legacy_tool, and drilldowns to raw events. Splunk ITSI users may attach entity import jobs keyed on normalized_host when KPIs consume the same indexes, but this UC remains valuable without premium modules.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation with a ten-minute schedule during production steady state, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate correlation_key rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action, tools, and heartbeat_age_min in pager narratives so incident commanders open the correct NMS console and forwarding path without re-inventing joins under stress.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans six legacy lanes—Nagios, Zabbix, Icinga2, SolarWinds NetFlow, Nimsoft, and SCOM—so a silent add-on cannot mask a still-active competitor tool screaming in another index. coalesce() ladders normalize host and check naming drift at ingest. eventstats measures how many distinct tools touch each normalized_host before correlation_key sorting. streamstats detects rapid transitions where one tool reports critical while another reports clear within the same correlation bucket, feeding transition_conflict hints. stats aggregates min and max numeric severity per correlation_key for spread-based reconciliation when tools disagree without a clean pairwise sequence. join enriches with Alerts data model recency via tstats for false-calm checks against CIM-normalized alert traffic, then joins inventory for owner_team routing. case() maps conflict, duplication, silence, and CIM lag into page versus warn versus info tiers distinct from UC-13.3.5 storm counting or UC-13.3.15 collector queue math.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.1 legacy NMS lanes Nagios Zabbix Icinga SolarWinds Nimsoft SCOM ingest dedupe SOT heartbeat inventory join tstats Alerts earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) (sourcetype=nagios:nagios.log OR sourcetype=nagios:host_perfdata) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="nagios"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, dest, nagios_host, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, service_description, check_command, "nagios_check"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, state, nagios_state, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|down|2"),5, match(raw_sev,"warning|unknown|1"),3, match(raw_sev,"ok|up|0"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) (sourcetype=zabbix:event OR sourcetype=zabbix:problem) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="zabbix"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(trigger_name, name, item_key, problem_name, "zabbix_item"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, status, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"disaster|high"),5, match(raw_sev,"average|warning"),3, match(raw_sev,"information|ok|resolved"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=icinga2:event earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="icinga2"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, check_name, "icinga_check"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(state, severity, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|2"),5, match(raw_sev,"warning|1"),3, match(raw_sev,"ok|0"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=solarwinds:netflow earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="solarwinds_npm"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, src_ip, dest, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(interface, flow_category, "npm_netflow_lane"))))
      | eval raw_sev="npm_netflow_presence"
      | eval sev_num=2
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=nimsoft:alarm earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="nimsoft_uim"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(robot, origin, host, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(probe, metric_source, alarm_msg, "nimsoft_probe"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, nim_sev, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|5"),5, match(raw_sev,"major|warning|4"),3, match(raw_sev,"clear|ok|0"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=scom:alert earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="scom"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(MonitoringObjectDisplayName, host, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(AlertName, monitor_display_name, "scom_monitor"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(Severity, resolution_state, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|error"),5, match(raw_sev,"warning|information"),3, match(raw_sev,"closed|resolved"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
| where len(normalized_host)>1 AND len(check_key)>0
| eval correlation_key=md5(strcat(normalized_host,"|",check_key))
| eventstats dc(legacy_tool) AS tools_seen_on_host BY normalized_host
| sort 0 correlation_key legacy_tool - _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(sev_num) AS prev_sev last(legacy_tool) AS prev_tool BY correlation_key
| eval transition_conflict=if(isnotnull(prev_tool) AND prev_tool!=legacy_tool AND prev_sev>=4 AND sev_num<=2,1,0)
| stats latest(_time) AS last_event max(sev_num) AS max_sev min(sev_num) AS min_sev dc(legacy_tool) AS tool_dc values(legacy_tool) AS tools values(raw_sev) AS state_samples max(transition_conflict) AS transition_conflict count AS evt_count first(normalized_host) AS normalized_host first(check_key) AS check_key first(tools_seen_on_host) AS tools_seen_on_host BY correlation_key
| eval has_conflict=if(transition_conflict>0 OR (tool_dc>1 AND max_sev>=4 AND min_sev<=2),1,0)
| eval dup_fanout=if(tool_dc>1 AND has_conflict==0,1,0)
| eval heartbeat_age_min=round((now()-last_event)/60,2)
| eval silent_tool_risk=if(heartbeat_age_min>120 AND max_sev>=4,1,0)
| eventstats median(evt_count) AS fleet_evt_median
| join type=left max=0 normalized_host
    [| tstats latest(_time) AS dm_last FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
     | rename Alerts.dest AS normalized_host
     | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(normalized_host)) ]
| eval dm_gap_min=if(isnotnull(dm_last), round((now()-dm_last)/60,2), null())
| join type=left max=0 normalized_host
    [| inputlookup legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv
     | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(canonical_host, host_fqdn, ip_primary, ""))))
     | fields normalized_host owner_team environment nms_tier monitoring_role ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| eval nms_tier=coalesce(nms_tier,"standard")
| eval monitoring_role=coalesce(monitoring_role,"unspecified")
| eval severity=case(has_conflict==1,"page", silent_tool_risk==1 OR evt_count>=8,"warn", dup_fanout==1,"warn", isnotnull(dm_gap_min) AND dm_gap_min>240 AND max_sev>=4,"warn", true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="page","reconcile_conflicting_severity_across_nms_then_update_cmdb_aliases", severity=="warn" AND dup_fanout==1,"collapse_duplicate_routes_and_align_host_correlation_keys", severity=="warn" AND silent_tool_risk==1,"restore_forwarder_snmp_trap_or_syslog_path_and_validate_ta_inputs", severity=="warn","compare_cim_alerts_recency_to_legacy_lanes_for_false_calm", true(),"continue_baseline_calibration_for_nms_fleet")
| table last_event normalized_host check_key tools tool_dc max_sev min_sev has_conflict dup_fanout evt_count heartbeat_age_min dm_gap_min fleet_evt_median owner_team environment nms_tier monitoring_role severity recommended_action correlation_key
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-monitoring-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = NMS reconcile $result.severity$ $result.normalized_host$ tools=$result.tools$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service level objectives, materialize per-lane summaries into an hourly summary index keyed on correlation_key and legacy_tool, widen alert searches to earliest=-90m on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For extremely chatty Zabbix triggers, consider raising the evt_count warn threshold after baseline review.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the authoritative NMS consoles. Open Nagios XI or Core status.cgi views for the host and service named in Splunk normalized_host and check_key, then compare state and last check time to Splunk last_event within one collection interval. When they disagree, verify Universal Forwarder connectivity, file tail offsets, syslog relay hops, and indexer clock skew before blaming business logic.

Zabbix validation: locate the trigger and latest events in the Zabbix UI, confirm problem severity matches sev_num mapping, and verify host interfaces match inventory canonical_host values. Icinga validation: use icinga2 console or API queries for the same host and service pairs. SolarWinds validation: confirm the node and interface referenced in NetFlow or NPM context still exists and that the collector service is running on the forwarding host.

Nimsoft validation: open Operator Console or Infrastructure Manager views for the robot and probe cited in state_samples. SCOM validation: inspect Operations Manager active alerts for MonitoringObjectDisplayName and cross-check ResolutionState transitions against Splunk min_sev and max_sev spread.

CIM cross-check: when dm_gap_min looks large while legacy tools show critical, confirm Alerts acceleration, verify dest field extraction, and ensure unrelated security alert traffic is not drowning signal. Synthetic validation: inject a controlled test service critical in a lab Nagios instance, confirm observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation surfaces a row with non-null recommended_action within twenty minutes, then clear the test and confirm severity returns to info.

Negative test: on hosts under approved maintenance with tagged silence, confirm warn-tier heartbeat logic does not page when maintenance_window_id is present in raw events or joined lookups. Fleet spot-check weekly: compare Splunk tool_dc distribution to CMDB monitoring_role coverage to catch inventory drift.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Nagios forwarder stops tailing nagios.log after logrotate without copytruncate: restore tailing paths, replay missed lines under change control, and validate file descriptor limits on the forwarder host.

Case 2 — Zabbix proxy partition loses database connectivity: events stall on the proxy while the server looks healthy; repair proxy DB links, verify Housekeeping settings, and compare proxy-lastaccess fields to Splunk gaps.

Case 3 — Icinga 2 satellite zone stops accepting config: masters show stale checks while satellites queue; replay config deployment, validate TLS trust between zones, and confirm feature flags after upgrades.

Case 4 — SolarWinds NPM collector CPU pegs and NetFlow stops while SNMP polls continue: scale collector hardware, redistribute flow exporters, and validate UDP buffer drops at the OS level.

Case 5 — Nimsoft hub queue backs up after WAN flap: drain hub spool directories under procedure, validate tunnel connectivity, and confirm robot versions after certificate rotation.

Case 6 — SCOM management server resource pool imbalance: workflows lag for specific management packs; rebalance pool membership, review SQL maintenance, and validate RunAs accounts.

Case 7 — Hostname mismatch between short names and FQDN causes false source-of-truth conflict: update legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv aliases, rebuild correlation_key tests, and teach forwarders consistent host naming.

Case 8 — Duplicate notifications via email and syslog double evt_count: route a single canonical path per tool into Splunk, dedupe at source, and document allowed fan-out receivers.

Case 9 — Time zone skew makes transition_conflict fire during orderly failover drills: align source and Splunk time zones, widen streamstats windows during drills, and tag change metadata.

Case 10 — CIM Alerts acceleration disabled makes dm_gap_min unreliable: re-enable acceleration, backfill summaries, and temporarily lower reliance on tstats join until green.

Case 11 — Maintenance silence mis-tagged as outage: enforce maintenance_calendar joins for heartbeat suppression and audit teams that pause checks without tickets.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists six NMS arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; closing table lists twenty analyst-visible columns including fleet_evt_median for fleet context.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.3.1 legacy NMS lanes Nagios Zabbix Icinga SolarWinds Nimsoft SCOM ingest dedupe SOT heartbeat inventory join tstats Alerts earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) (sourcetype=nagios:nagios.log OR sourcetype=nagios:host_perfdata) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="nagios"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, dest, nagios_host, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, service_description, check_command, "nagios_check"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, state, nagios_state, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|down|2"),5, match(raw_sev,"warning|unknown|1"),3, match(raw_sev,"ok|up|0"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) (sourcetype=zabbix:event OR sourcetype=zabbix:problem) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="zabbix"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(trigger_name, name, item_key, problem_name, "zabbix_item"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, status, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"disaster|high"),5, match(raw_sev,"average|warning"),3, match(raw_sev,"information|ok|resolved"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=icinga2:event earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="icinga2"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, check_name, "icinga_check"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(state, severity, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|2"),5, match(raw_sev,"warning|1"),3, match(raw_sev,"ok|0"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=solarwinds:netflow earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="solarwinds_npm"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, src_ip, dest, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(interface, flow_category, "npm_netflow_lane"))))
      | eval raw_sev="npm_netflow_presence"
      | eval sev_num=2
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=nimsoft:alarm earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="nimsoft_uim"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(robot, origin, host, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(probe, metric_source, alarm_msg, "nimsoft_probe"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, nim_sev, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|5"),5, match(raw_sev,"major|warning|4"),3, match(raw_sev,"clear|ok|0"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
    [ search (index=monitoring OR index=legacy_alerts) sourcetype=scom:alert earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval legacy_tool="scom"
      | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(MonitoringObjectDisplayName, host, ""))))
      | eval check_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(AlertName, monitor_display_name, "scom_monitor"))))
      | eval raw_sev=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(Severity, resolution_state, ""))))
      | eval sev_num=case(match(raw_sev,"critical|error"),5, match(raw_sev,"warning|information"),3, match(raw_sev,"closed|resolved"),1, true(),2)
      | fields _time legacy_tool normalized_host check_key sev_num raw_sev ]
| where len(normalized_host)>1 AND len(check_key)>0
| eval correlation_key=md5(strcat(normalized_host,"|",check_key))
| eventstats dc(legacy_tool) AS tools_seen_on_host BY normalized_host
| sort 0 correlation_key legacy_tool - _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(sev_num) AS prev_sev last(legacy_tool) AS prev_tool BY correlation_key
| eval transition_conflict=if(isnotnull(prev_tool) AND prev_tool!=legacy_tool AND prev_sev>=4 AND sev_num<=2,1,0)
| stats latest(_time) AS last_event max(sev_num) AS max_sev min(sev_num) AS min_sev dc(legacy_tool) AS tool_dc values(legacy_tool) AS tools values(raw_sev) AS state_samples max(transition_conflict) AS transition_conflict count AS evt_count first(normalized_host) AS normalized_host first(check_key) AS check_key first(tools_seen_on_host) AS tools_seen_on_host BY correlation_key
| eval has_conflict=if(transition_conflict>0 OR (tool_dc>1 AND max_sev>=4 AND min_sev<=2),1,0)
| eval dup_fanout=if(tool_dc>1 AND has_conflict==0,1,0)
| eval heartbeat_age_min=round((now()-last_event)/60,2)
| eval silent_tool_risk=if(heartbeat_age_min>120 AND max_sev>=4,1,0)
| eventstats median(evt_count) AS fleet_evt_median
| join type=left max=0 normalized_host
    [| tstats latest(_time) AS dm_last FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
     | rename Alerts.dest AS normalized_host
     | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(normalized_host)) ]
| eval dm_gap_min=if(isnotnull(dm_last), round((now()-dm_last)/60,2), null())
| join type=left max=0 normalized_host
    [| inputlookup legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv
     | eval normalized_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(canonical_host, host_fqdn, ip_primary, ""))))
     | fields normalized_host owner_team environment nms_tier monitoring_role ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| eval nms_tier=coalesce(nms_tier,"standard")
| eval monitoring_role=coalesce(monitoring_role,"unspecified")
| eval severity=case(has_conflict==1,"page", silent_tool_risk==1 OR evt_count>=8,"warn", dup_fanout==1,"warn", isnotnull(dm_gap_min) AND dm_gap_min>240 AND max_sev>=4,"warn", true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="page","reconcile_conflicting_severity_across_nms_then_update_cmdb_aliases", severity=="warn" AND dup_fanout==1,"collapse_duplicate_routes_and_align_host_correlation_keys", severity=="warn" AND silent_tool_risk==1,"restore_forwarder_snmp_trap_or_syslog_path_and_validate_ta_inputs", severity=="warn","compare_cim_alerts_recency_to_legacy_lanes_for_false_calm", true(),"continue_baseline_calibration_for_nms_fleet")
| table last_event normalized_host check_key tools tool_dc max_sev min_sev has_conflict dup_fanout evt_count heartbeat_age_min dm_gap_min fleet_evt_median owner_team environment nms_tier monitoring_role severity recommended_action correlation_key
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.severity) AS sev latest(Alerts.signature) AS sig count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS alert_host
| join type=left max=0 alert_host [| inputlookup legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv | eval alert_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(canonical_host, host_fqdn, "")))) | fields alert_host owner_team environment]
| table alert_host sev sig count owner_team environment
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Alerts summaries for dest and severity fields mapped from legacy NMS sourcetypes when props.conf aliases align Nagios, Zabbix, Icinga, Nimsoft, and SCOM extractions; retain thirty days for quarterly reconciliation attestations.
visualization: Severity-colored table by normalized_host and correlation_key with drilldowns to raw nagios, zabbix, icinga2, solarwinds, nimsoft, and scom events; sparkline of heartbeat_age_min; single-value tiles for tool_dc and has_conflict counts by environment.

references.title: Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Nagios Core
Splunkbase — Icinga2 Passive Check Result Alert Action
Splunk Lantern — Combining multiple data sources in SPL

knownFalsePositives: Planned Nagios or Icinga maintenance windows suppress checks legitimately and can look like ingestion failure until maintenance_calendar metadata aligns; require ticket tags before muting. Zabbix flapping triggers that auto-resolve within seconds may create artificial transition_conflict rows; widen correlation windows or filter ultra-short problems in props. SolarWinds NetFlow gaps during exporter reconfiguration are common while SNMP remains up; do not treat NetFlow silence alone as host down without NPM context. SCOM alert auto-resolution storms after management pack updates can min_sev to cleared while operators still see console noise; pair with ResolutionState timelines. Nimsoft probe restarts emit brief clear-critical oscillations that resemble conflict; ignore single-minute spikes without second-source confirmation. CMDB inventory drift where canonical_host stops matching robot or MonitoringObjectDisplayName produces false dedupe keys; refresh lookups nightly. Duplicate syslog forwarding through redundant relays doubles evt_count without doubling physical faults; dedupe at the edge. Lab chaos tests that pause Universal Forwarders will trigger silent_tool_risk; isolate lab indexes. Certificate rotations on Zabbix or Icinga APIs can stall pollers temporarily; overlap validity windows. Time zone changes around daylight saving boundaries can skew heartbeat_age_min until sources stabilize.
hardware: Applies to on-premises or colocated monitoring servers, SNMP trap collectors, syslog relays, Windows SCOM management servers, and Linux forwarders tailing Nagios logs or polling Zabbix and Icinga APIs.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.3.5 alert storm detection across paging vendors, UC-13.3.15 OpenTelemetry Collector backpressure analytics, deep NPM flow analytics unrelated to alert consolidation, or automated CMDB updates; it produces evidence and routing guidance for governed reconciliation workflows.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab estate, emit overlapping Nagios CRITICAL and Zabbix OK rows for the same inventory host within ten minutes, confirm observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation returns page with has_conflict=1 and a non-null recommended_action within two scheduled windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when consoles agree, forwarders show steady volume per sourcetype, and inventory keys align, filtering the saved search to page and warn should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs under quiet steady state.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_3_1_nms_ingest_reconciliation on a ten-minute schedule; exported CSV joined to legacy_nms_host_inventory.csv; dashboard drilldowns archived to a restricted observability evidence index.
